feat: make LSP code actions configurable via setup() (#4)

Move the hardcoded LSP code action definitions out of
lsp-code-actions.lua and into config.lua as a default
lsp_code_actions list, matching the pattern used for keymaps.

Add Aiwaku.LspCodeAction type to types.lua with a required
title and optional prompt prefix field. Extend Aiwaku.Config
with the new lsp_code_actions field.

Refactor lsp-code-actions.lua to read from
state.config.lsp_code_actions at generator call time instead
of maintaining a hardcoded list. Guards against missing/empty
titles with a WARN notification and skips those entries.
Add null-ls load guard that returns {} with a WARN when
null-ls is not available.

Users can now override or extend the default action list
during setup():

  require("aiwaku").setup({
    lsp_code_actions = {
      { title = "Send to Aiwaku" },
      { title = "AI: write tests", prompt = "write tests for:" },
    },
  })

Update README.md and doc/aiwaku.nvim.txt to document the
new lsp_code_actions option and how to override the defaults.

commit.message:
fix: last 3 case-sensitive state comparisons — root cause of stale issues

The initial issue load in registerWeb() and the milestone state check
in ws-server.ts both compared i.state === 'closed' (lowercase) against
gh CLI's 'CLOSED' (uppercase). This was the ACTUAL root cause — the
active sprint's initial issue list was built with the wrong comparison,
and since it's the active sprint, handleSprintIssues always served
this stale data via getIssues() callback.

Fixed all 3 remaining locations. Total: 6 locations across 3 files
had this bug — now all use .toLowerCase().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: issue cache always fetches from GitHub — removes stale saved-state path

Root cause: loadSprint() tried saved state first, mapping all issues
to 'planned' regardless of actual GitHub state. Even after closing
an issue on GitHub, the cache would show it as 'planned' because the
saved state was created at planning time and never updated.

Fix: loadSprint() now always fetches from GitHub via fetchFromGitHub().
Saved state was unreliable for issue status — it only knew about the
planning snapshot, not runtime state changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): redirect to session hub when no sessions exist (#122)

When the terminal page (`/terminal`) has no sessions — on initial load,
after the last session ends, or when polling detects all sessions are
gone — it previously showed a dead-end "No sessions" label with no way
forward.

Now it redirects to the session hub (`/`) using `location.replace()` so
users land on the proper empty state with a "+ New Session" button,
without polluting browser history.

**Changes:**
- Replace three `window.location.href` dead-end states in
`terminal.html` with `window.location.replace('/')`
- Add `test/e2e-empty-sessions.test.js` — Playwright E2E test covering
the redirect

Closes #121

* fix: resolve Stage 3 FICA gate deadlock caused by Inngest replay model

In Inngest, step.run() callbacks are NOT re-executed when a step result
is memoized (already completed). Setting context.documentsComplete = true
inside the 'mandate-verified' step callback meant this value was always
lost on replay, so Stage 3's ficaPromise always created a waitForEvent
for a second upload/fica.received event that never arrived.

Fix: return { documentsComplete: true } from the 'mandate-verified' step
and use mandateVerified.documentsComplete in Stage 3's ficaPromise gate.
Also replaced context.mandateType/mandateVolume (dead side effects inside
step callbacks) with mandateInfo.mandateType/mandateVolume from the
already-correct determine-mandate step return value.

Verified end-to-end: procurement CLEARED → Stage 3 FICA gate resolves
immediately via Promise.resolve (ficaSource: "stage2_documents_already_complete")
→ ITC/Sanctions/Reporter run → Stage 4 → human APPROVED → Stage 5.

* CU-869cazc7v Added-Missing-Workflow-gates

Add missing event emitters and authenticated workflow action endpoints for contract review and financial statements confirmation, expose kill switch/contract/risk controls in the applicant UI, and harden contract decision waiting with form-type filtering to prevent false terminal failures.

- Workflow gate: wait-absa-completed (Stage 5)

* add Inngest Workflow Audit report and UI handling documentation

Introduce a comprehensive audit report for the Inngest workflow system, detailing workflow functions, step inventory, and applicant checks. Additionally, document the UI architecture and its mapping to workflow stages, highlighting critical gaps in the current implementation. This commit enhances the understanding of the workflow processes and identifies areas for improvement.

* refactor contract gate flow and add dedicated review route

Move contract review out of the applicant reviews tab into a dedicated route, add an ABSA mock handoff action, and shift applicant contract delivery to happen only after two-factor final approval.

* Update documentation with new executive summary and workflow details

- Added "Executive Summary" section to provide an overview of the StratCol Control Tower platform.
- Enhanced workflow documentation by clarifying procurement and AI verification processes, including changes to input wait gates and synthesis of results.
- Updated risk review model to reflect a single decision gate and improved context for check-level statuses.
- Included quality checks for document uploads in the API reference documentation.

* Remove outdated Firecrawl Check Agent Contracts and procurement fallback framework documentation. These files are no longer relevant to the current architecture and processes, streamlining the documentation for better clarity and focus on active features.

* Remove Inngest skill from incorrect directory

* Updates Gitignore files to exclude PNGs and introduces a new documentation plan.

This change primarily modifies the repository's .gitignore files to prevent .png images from being tracked by Git. Additionally, a new .cursor plan document has been added, detailing a set of planned updates for project documentation and its integration with Mintlify.

* Refactor document type handling in events and document aggregator

- Introduced a new type import for DocumentType in events.ts to enhance type safety.
- Updated document aggregation logic in document-aggregator.ts to validate document types using Zod's DocumentTypeSchema, ensuring only valid types are processed and emitted.

* Secure workflow-critical API routes with authentication.

Add shared API auth helpers and enforce Clerk or bearer-token checks on applicant, workflow, quote approval, signal, and resolve-error endpoints to prevent unauthenticated event injection and financial actions.

* Add validation schemas for Inngest events

- Introduced multiple Zod schemas for validating various Inngest event types, including onboarding leads, workflow terminations, document uploads, and more.
- Each schema defines the structure and constraints for the corresponding event data, ensuring type safety and data integrity.
- Added comprehensive test cases to validate the correctness of the schemas and their handling of valid and invalid data inputs.

* Add API authentication utilities

- Introduced a new module for API authentication in `api-auth.ts`, implementing functions to handle user authentication and bearer token validation.
- Added `requireAuth` to enforce user authentication and return appropriate responses for unauthorized access.
- Implemented `requireAuthOrBearer` to allow access via either user authentication or a valid bearer token, enhancing security for API endpoints.
- Included utility functions for extracting and validating tokens from request headers.

* Update README to reflect platform rebranding and feature enhancements

- Renamed the project from "SCOL Watchtower" to "StratCol Onboard Control Tower" for clarity.
- Revised the overview and features sections to highlight the zero-middleware architecture, 6-stage control tower process, and enhanced verification and risk management capabilities.
- Updated the tech stack to include Next.js 16 and additional technologies.
- Improved the getting started instructions and added commands for better user guidance.

* Remove V24 Integration Service implementation

- Deleted the entire V24 service file, which included functions for creating client profiles, scheduling training sessions, and sending welcome packs.
- This change removes the mock implementation for V24 core system integration, streamlining the codebase.

* fix build by standardizing Google GenAI payloads

Replace unsupported schema-based generateContent calls with JSON schema config + explicit parsing so Next.js TypeScript build passes again.

* Update ProcureCheck integration and environment configurations

- Changed the `ENABLE_PROCURECHECK_LIVE` setting in `.env.example` from false to true to enable live ProcureCheck functionality.
- Updated the `Credit Check API` email field for a specific user to reflect a new domain.
- Refactored the `ApplicantForm` component to use a more generic test mode flag instead of the Mockaroo-specific one.
- Renamed V24 integration references to `agreementContract` across various files, including event types and API response schemas.
- Adjusted ITC service to integrate with ProcureCheck instead of Experian, updating related configurations and error handling.
- Enhanced AI model configurations to utilize Google GenAI, updating environment variable requirements accordingly.

* standardize Gemini client to use GOOGLE_GENAI_KEY

Create a shared GenAI client that passes GOOGLE_GENAI_KEY explicitly and migrate quote/agent services to use it so runtime API key detection is consistent.

* switch Gemini model IDs to supported stable variants

Replace unavailable gemini-3-flash identifiers with Gemini 2.5 model IDs that support structured output and generateContent on the current API endpoint.

* migrate structured AI calls to Gemini Interactions API

Replace generateContent-based structured calls with interactions.create + response_format, and centralize parsing/validation for quote and agent pipelines.

* use native PDF document input for FICA and validation

Send base64 PDFs as application/pdf inlineData to Gemini document understanding so structured analysis can use full page context instead of placeholder text.

* use Gemini PDF input in Stage 3 risk analysis

Pass Stage 3 bank statement documents into aggregated analysis and analyze them as application/pdf inline data so risk scoring uses native document understanding.

* update Mintlify docs for Stage 3 PDF analysis flow

Document Stage 3 native PDF bank-statement processing, add risk review queue context for document mode, and align changelog entries with current Gemini client and structured analysis updates.

Introduce a new file, coderepomix.md, which serves as a packed representation of the codebase for AI consumption, summarizing key repository contents and structure.
